title: The Referee Arrives
lines: Two crowds keep shouting different numbers across one thin river.
Upriver, the smokehouses are quiet. The kings keep failing.
The trawl fleet hates salmon in its nets too. The ocean drives much of this.
Counting the catch took an observer weeks.
Now a Noah model reads the net cameras in hours.
Trained on eighty five thousand labeled fish. Ninety seven percent spotted. Still research.
This month a Senate bill demanded numbers everyone can trust.
The fight finally has a referee. But referees settle arguments. They do not refill rivers.
At Redoubt Lake, the Sitka Tribe of Alaska built its own weir. Their river. Their count.
The machine won't refill the river. But whose count do you trust?
notes: Spoken 'Noah' is the phonetic respelling of NOAA (display spelling restored by caption_fixups). Numbers spoken as words, drawn as numerals in the scene annotations. No em or en dashes anywhere. 125 words at a brisk read lands ~50-56s.
